perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, (i) the term “ Representative ” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, general partners, managers, members, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants), and with the written consent (including via email, which such consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed) of the Company or as referenced on the attached Schedule A of this Agreement any consultants and potential financing sources
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, (i) the term “ Representative ” means, as to any person, such person’s affiliates and its and their respective directors, officers, general partners, managers, members, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants), and with the verbal consent of the Company any consultants and potential financing sources
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'written consent' to 'verbal consent' for including consultants/financing sources as 'Representatives' creates ambiguity and potential conflict.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware's Statute of Frauds requires certain agreements, particularly those involving significant financial transactions or long-term obligations, to be in writing to be enforceable. Allowing verbal consent for key representatives could render aspects of the NDA unenforceable.

Contracts Subchapter I. General Provisions Â§ 2701. Joint and several contracts. An obligation or written contract of several persons shall be joint and several, unless otherwise expressed. (a) All bonds, specialties, and notes in writing, payable to any person, or order, or assigns, may be assigned, or indorsed, and the assignee, or indorsee, his or her executors, administrators, or assigns, may again assign, or indorse the same, as often as desired. The assignees, or indorsees, or their executors, or administrators, may, in their own name, sue for and recover the money due on the bonds, specialties, or notes. All assignments of bonds or specialties shall be under hand and seal, and executed before at least one credible witness. (b) Assignors, or indorsers, or their executors, or administrators may not release or discharge any sum due by the bonds, specialties, or notes, after the date of the assignment, and no release, receipt, or discharge from him, her or them made after the date of the assignment shall be good or available. perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'written consent' for including consultants/financing sources as 'Representatives,' while the changed text requires 'verbal consent.' The Law Explanation suggests that Delaware's Statute of Frauds requires certain financial agreements to be in writing to be enforceable. Scraped Law Snippet 1 discusses written contracts and assignments of bonds/specialties under seal, implying a preference for written agreements for legal enforceability in Delaware. Although the snippet doesn't directly address consent for representatives, the general emphasis on written agreements, combined with the Law Explanation, suggests that changing 'written consent' to 'verbal consent' could weaken the enforceability of the agreement, creating a legal contradiction by potentially violating the Statute of Frauds.

perturbation.original_text: The Company reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to determine what information it will provide or withhold (and, if provided, the form thereof), as well as the times and locations at which it elects to make such information available.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to determine what information it will provide or withhold (and, if provided, the form thereof), as well as the times and locations at which it elects to make such information available. The Company may withhold information based on subjective beliefs.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'The Company may withhold information based on subjective beliefs' allows for arbitrary withholding of information, undermining the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_citation: Delaware law, Guth v. Loft, Inc., 5 A.2d 503, 510 (Del.Supr. 1939)
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract. Allowing the Company to withhold information based on 'subjective beliefs' contradicts this covenant, as it permits arbitrary and potentially self-serving behavior that frustrates the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.justification: The added sentence, 'The Company may withhold information based on subjective beliefs,' directly contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, as it allows the company to arbitrarily withhold information. The Law Explanation states Delaware law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract, and the added text enables self-serving behavior, frustrating the purpose of the NDA. While there are no scraped law snippets to reference, the perturbation explanation, law explanation and the definitions support the finding of a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that for a period of eighteen (18) months from and after the date hereof (the “ Standstill Period ”), neither you nor any of your affiliates, directly or indirectly, shall: (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, whether by means of purchase or otherwise, any (i) securities (or any interest therein or right thereto) having statutory, organic or contractual voting power, whether or not contingent or upon exercise, conversion or exchange (“ Voting Securities ”), of the Company or (ii) assets or businesses of the Company or of any division or operating unit of the Company
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that for a period of eighteen (18) months from and after the date hereof (the “ Standstill Period ”), neither you nor any of your affiliates, directly or indirectly, shall: (a) acquire, offer to acquire, or agree to acquire, whether by means of purchase or otherwise, any (i) securities (or any interest therein or right thereto) having statutory, organic or contractual voting power, whether or not contingent or upon exercise, conversion or exchange (“ Voting Securities ”), of the Company or (ii) assets or businesses of the Company or of any division or operating unit of the Company unless otherwise permitted by the SEC.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'unless otherwise permitted by the SEC' to the standstill provision introduces uncertainty, as the SEC does not typically grant individual exemptions to standstill agreements. This weakens the provision.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Williams Act amendments to the Securities Exchange Act of 1934
perturbation.law_explanation: The Williams Act governs tender offers and large-scale acquisitions of securities. While the SEC enforces these regulations, it doesn't 'permit' or 'exempt' individual standstill agreements in the manner suggested by the modified text. The SEC's role is to ensure compliance with disclosure requirements and prevent market manipulation, not to provide waivers for contractual standstill provisions.

Periodical and other reports (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security- (1) such information and documents (and suc
perturbation.justification: The added phrase "unless otherwise permitted by the SEC" creates a legal contradiction. The Law Explanation states that the SEC's role is to ensure compliance and prevent market manipulation, not to provide waivers for contractual standstill provisions. This addition weakens the standstill provision by implying that the SEC can grant exceptions to it, which is not within its typical purview. The snippets do not provide direct support, but the Law Explanation, in conjunction with the Perturbation Explanation, highlights the misalignment. The addition introduces uncertainty and a potential loophole that undermines the original intent of the standstill agreement, making the contract less enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the internal procedural and substantive laws of the State of Delaware, without reference to the conflict of law principles of that state.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of Delaware, including its conflict of law principles.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'internal procedural and substantive' and adding 'including its conflict of law principles' creates uncertainty. Conflict of law principles could lead to the application of another jurisdiction's laws, contradicting the intention of applying only Delaware law.
perturbation.law_explanation: While parties can generally choose the governing law of their contract, including the phrase “including its conflict of law principles” opens the door for a court to potentially apply the laws of a different jurisdiction if the conflict of laws analysis suggests it. This directly contradicts the original intent of specifying Delaware law exclusively and introduces uncertainty about which laws will ultimately govern the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that Delaware law governs the agreement 'without reference to the conflict of law principles of that state,' aiming for a straightforward application of Delaware's internal laws. The changed text, by 'including its conflict of law principles,' introduces the possibility that a court might apply laws from another jurisdiction if Delaware's conflict of law rules so dictate. This directly contradicts the initial intention of applying only Delaware law and creates uncertainty about which laws will ultimately govern, as stated in the Law Explanation. The absence of relevant scraped law snippets does not negate the contradiction created by this specific shift in contractual language, as the Perturbation Explanation outlines how this change undermines the original intent.
